title: Pouteria endlicheri
taxonomy: Pouteria endlicheri is classified in the kingdom Plantae, specifically within the family Sapotaceae. This plant is further classified in the order Ebenales, which is a group of plants known for their Sapotaceae family members. The family Sapotaceae is divided into several genera, and Pouteria endlicheri belongs to the genus Pouteria. The plant's classification also includes being a species within the genus Pouteria, and the specific species is Pouteria endlicheri. It is also known by its synonym, Planchonella endlicheri, which is another name for this species.

intro: Pouteria endlicheri, also known as the Queensland tallowwood, is a large evergreen tree native to the tropical rainforests of northern Australia. The bark and leaves of Pouteria endlicheri have been traditionally used by indigenous Australians for medicinal purposes, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can aid in wound healing and the treatment of various skin conditions.
